reviewer: weekly-hardening-routine (headless Chromium 141 via CDP; no chrome-devtools-mcp in cloud env)

spec_match.rationale: Exercises the explainer's two handler paths (respondWith(fetch) and fallback) with a real SW and real PerformanceNavigationTiming; honestly states auto-preload has no direct JS probe.

interactive.rationale: Register/unregister, mode select, delay slider and measure button all drive a real controlled iframe navigation; verified working in headless Chromium 141 (TTFB 314.8ms at 300ms delay, fallback 7.1ms).

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index card promises real timing readouts and that is what the page does.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles unsupported serviceWorker, uncontrolled-first-load race and fallback mode, but has no way to show the serialised (auto-preload off) baseline for comparison in the same browser.

conformance.rationale: New feature-level suite probes the exact anchor surfaces (workerStart, CacheStorage, register); executed in-browser with no runner errors.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Scripted CDP run: register -> measure -> mode flip -> keyboard on slider -> unregister; before/after full-page screenshots read and inspected; only console noise is the sandbox-blocked Google Fonts request.

telemetry_health.rationale: Page asserts registered/measured/unsupported branches via showcaseTelemetry, but local KV endpoint was not queried during this review.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables only; dark buttons keep light text; readout table on paper background passes AA.

accessibility.rationale: Native buttons/select/range with labels, role=status aria-live readouts, keyboard-verified slider (ArrowRight moved 300->350ms).

summary: Real service worker timing lab verified end-to-end in headless Chromium 141; honest heuristic verdict language.

openQuestions.title: Show the serialised baseline with auto-preload disabled
openQuestions.detail: Run the same measurement in a Chromium launched with --disable-features=ServiceWorkerAutoPreload and present the two TTFB distributions side by side, so the overlap verdict is anchored against a real negative control rather than only the delay heuristic.
